question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 61.00% on 2026-03-26 for the Metaculus question "Will Keir Starmer cease to be Prime Minister of the UK during 2026?"?
summary: The CP on the Starmer question is currently exactly at the 61.00% threshold. The question asks whether it will be strictly above 61.00% on March 26, roughly 12 days away. The CP has been oscillating in the 58-65% range since mid-February with a recent mean of ~61.9%, but dropped sharply from 65% to 61% in the most recent week. The Iran/Middle East crisis appears to be stabilizing Starmer's position (making leadership change seem risky during turmoil), which pushes CP down. However, Manifold markets price the underlying question at ~65%, Starmer remains deeply unpopular (-50 approval, Labour at ~20%), and local elections in May keep leadership speculation alive. Monte Carlo simulations give 46-55% probability depending on assumptions about drift and mean-reversion. This is genuinely near a coin flip with a very slight lean toward NO given the recent downward momentum.

factors.description: CP currently exactly at the threshold (61.00%) - starting right at the boundary means roughly 50/50 purely from position
Recent downward momentum: CP dropped from 65% to 61% in one week, suggesting bearish drift driven by Iran crisis stabilization narrative
Manifold market at ~65% on same underlying question suggests informed markets think probability should be above 61%, creating upward pull
Recent CP mean since mid-February is ~61.9%, slightly above threshold - mean reversion favors crossing above
Iran/Middle East crisis stabilizes Starmer by making leadership change seem dangerous during turmoil, reducing probability of him leaving
Starmer deeply unpopular (-50 approval, Labour at ~20% in polls), May 7 local elections approaching - keeps leadership speculation and CP elevated
No major catalyst event expected before March 26 to decisively move CP; status quo stickiness near 61%

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.49 (49%)**

This is a genuinely close-to-coin-flip situation because the CP is sitting exactly at the 61.00% threshold. Key considerations:

- **Near the knife-edge**: The CP is exactly at 61.00%, and the resolution requires *strictly* above 61.00%. Any tiny move matters.
- **Recent downward momentum**: CP dropped 4 points (65% → 61%) in one week, driven partly by the Iran crisis stabilizing Starmer's position.
- **Opposing upward forces**: Manifold prices the underlying at ~65%, the recent CP average is ~61.9%, and Starmer's deep unpopularity plus upcoming May elections keep leadership risk alive.
- **Monte Carlo simulations**: Pure random walk gives ~46%, mean-reversion models give ~48-55%, scenario-weighted average ~46%.

I lean very slightly toward NO (49%) based on the recent downward momentum and the stabilizing effect of the Iran crisis on Starmer's position, but this is well within the uncertainty range.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/40967) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-26 15:49:17 is higher than 61.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 61.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-26 15:49:17, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
